skill.name: cdk-patterns
skill.description: Common AWS CDK patterns and constructs for building cloud infrastructure with TypeScript, Python, or Java. Use when designing reusable CDK stacks and L3 constructs.
skill.summary: Build production-ready AWS infrastructure using proven CDK patterns and reusable constructs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 'Dynamic function constructor' at line 53 is actually a CDK Lambda construct (new lambda.Function). The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code block delimiters (```typescript), not shell commands. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference description text, not actual crypto code. This skill contains only documentation and example CDK TypeScript code with no executable security risks.

content.value_statement: Design production-ready AWS infrastructure using CDK constructs. Apply proven patterns for serverless APIs, containers, and data pipelines with reusable L2/L3 constructs.
actual_capabilities: Generate reusable CDK L2 and L3 constructs for common infrastructure patterns
Design multi-stack CDK applications with proper separation of concerns
Implement serverless API patterns with API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB
Create ECS service patterns for containerized workloads
Apply IAM least privilege principles to all generated roles and policies
Enable monitoring with CloudWatch alarms and X-Ray tracing by default
limitations: Does not generate raw CloudFormation templates without CDK abstraction
Not suitable for Terraform-specific infrastructure workflows
Requires AWS CDK framework knowledge for effective use
Does not handle one-off CLI resource creation without CDK stacks

use_cases.title: Serverless API Development
use_cases.description: Build production-ready serverless APIs using API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB with proper IAM permissions and monitoring enabled.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building scalable APIs on AWS

use_cases.title: Reusable Infrastructure Constructs
use_cases.description: Create organization-standard L3 constructs that encode best practices for consistent infrastructure deployments across teams.

use_cases.title: CDK Code Review and Optimization
use_cases.description: Review existing CDK code for anti-patterns, security issues, and opportunities to use higher-level constructs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic CDK Stack Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CDK stack in TypeScript that deploys a Lambda function with an S3 bucket. Use L2 constructs, apply removal policies, and enable X-Ray tracing.

prompt_templates.title: Serverless API Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a reusable CDK construct for a serverless API with API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB. Include proper IAM permissions, environment variables, and CloudWatch alarms.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Stack Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-stack CDK application separating stateful resources (RDS, S3) from stateless compute (Lambda, ECS). Show how to pass references between stacks.

prompt_templates.title: CDK Code Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CDK stack for security anti-patterns, IAM over-permissions, and opportunities to use L2 constructs instead of L1. Suggest specific improvements.

output_examples.input: Create a serverless API with Lambda and DynamoDB
output_examples.output: Generates a complete CDK construct with API Gateway integration, Lambda function with proper runtime configuration, DynamoDB table with on-demand billing, IAM permissions using grantReadWriteData, and X-Ray tracing enabled for observability.

output_examples.input: Review my CDK stack for best practices
output_examples.output: Provides specific recommendations: replace CfnFunction with lambda.Function L2 construct, add RemovalPolicy.RETAIN for stateful resources, implement least privilege IAM using table.grantReadWriteData instead of inline policies, and add cdk.Tags for resource organization.

best_practices: Use L2 constructs over L1 (Cfn*) constructs for safer defaults and less boilerplate code
Separate stateful resources (databases, buckets) from stateless compute into different stacks for independent lifecycle management
Apply the principle of least privilege to all IAM roles using grant methods instead of broad inline policies
anti_patterns: Using L1 (Cfn*) constructs when L2 alternatives exist, resulting in more code and weaker safety guarantees
Hardcoding account IDs or regions instead of using cdk.Aws.ACCOUNT_ID and cdk.Aws.REGION for portability
Deploying stateful and stateless resources in the same stack, coupling their lifecycles unnecessarily

faq.question: What is the difference between L1 and L2 CDK constructs?
faq.answer: L1 constructs (Cfn*) are auto-generated from CloudFormation and map 1:1 to resources. L2 constructs provide higher-level abstractions with sensible defaults, validation, and helper methods. Always prefer L2 constructs when available.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets in CDK?
faq.answer: Use AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store. Reference secrets using secret.secretValueFromJson() and grant read access to Lambda functions using secret.grantRead(handler). Never hardcode secrets in your CDK code.

faq.question: What is the recommended stack separation strategy?
faq.answer: Separate stateful resources (databases, S3 buckets) from stateless compute (Lambda, ECS). This allows independent deployments and prevents accidental data loss when updating compute resources.

faq.question: How do I enable monitoring in my CDK stacks?
faq.answer: Enable X-Ray tracing on Lambda functions with tracing: lambda.Tracing.ACTIVE. Add CloudWatch alarms for error rates, latency, and throttling. Use CDK Monitoring constructs or aws-cdk-lib/aws-cloudwatch for alarm definitions.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Python or Java instead of TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. The patterns are language-agnostic. The skill can generate CDK code in TypeScript, Python, or Java. The underlying constructs and best practices remain the same across languages.

faq.question: How do I handle circular dependencies between stacks?
faq.answer: Extract shared resources into a dedicated base stack. Pass resource references (ARNs, IDs) between stacks using constructor props or cross-stack references via stack exports and Fn.importValue().
